title: Funding squeeze unwind signal research report
summary: Extreme funding combined with rising open interest can indicate crowded perpetual positioning. If price continuation stalls while funding remains expensive and OI later contracts, the window may identify a squeeze-unwind alert. The strategy should begin as a risk filter for carry and momentum systems because crowded positioning can reverse violently.
claims: Funding rate, open interest and mark price can be aligned into a repeatable crowded-positioning panel.
A squeeze unwind should require both crowding evidence and loss of price continuation, not just extreme funding.
The first version is more suitable for alerting and suppressing late entries than for automatic contrarian execution.
formulas: unwind_flag = funding_extreme and oi_change_pct turns negative after price continuation stalls
cost_items: Taker fee and slippage if converted from alert to entry.
Funding paid while waiting for unwind confirmation.
Opportunity cost from blocking profitable trend continuation trades.
Higher liquidation and gap risk during crowded stress windows.
failure_modes: Extreme funding persists longer than the holding budget and trend continues.
OI drops because positions close after the move is already over.
Funding and OI venue definitions differ across exchanges and cannot be mixed.
Contrarian entries during squeeze windows suffer adverse selection and high slippage.
evidence_notes: Operator fit: medium-high as an alert and late-entry filter; medium-low as a standalone contrarian trade.
Venue boundary: each exchange's OI contract definition and sampling interval must be fixed before cross-venue comparison.
Safety boundary: no high leverage, no automatic short squeeze or long squeeze entries, and no production-ready label before replay.
